# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.
# Category: sql_injection

function generateAdminReport(PDO $pdo): array
{
    $filters = $_POST ?? [];

    $status = isset($filters['status']) ? trim((string)$filters['status']) : '';
    $category = isset($filters['category']) ? trim((string)$filters['category']) : '';
    $dateFrom = isset($filters['date_from']) ? trim((string)$filters['date_from']) : '';
    $dateTo = isset($filters['date_to']) ? trim((string)$filters['date_to']) : '';

    $where = [];
    $params = [];

    if ($status !== '' && $status !== 'all') {
        $where[] = "r.status = :status";
        $params[':status'] = $status;
    }

    if ($category !== '' && $category !== 'all') {
        $where[] = "r.category = :category";
        $params[':category'] = $category;
    }

    if ($dateFrom !== '') {
        $where[] = "r.created_at >= :date_from";
        $params[':date_from'] = $dateFrom . (strlen($dateFrom) === 10 ? " 00:00:00" : "");
    }

    if ($dateTo !== '') {
        $where[] = "r.created_at <= :date_to";
        $params[':date_to'] = $dateTo . (strlen($dateTo) === 10 ? " 23:59:59" : "");
    }

    $sql = "
        SELECT
            r.id,
            r.title,
            r.status,
            r.category,
            r.created_at,
            r.updated_at
        FROM reports r
    ";

    if (!empty($where)) {
        $sql .= " WHERE " . implode(" AND ", $where);
    }

    $sql .= " ORDER BY r.created_at DESC";

    $stmt = $pdo->prepare($sql);
    foreach ($params as $key => $value) {
        $stmt->bindValue($key, $value);
    }
    $stmt->execute();

    $rows = $stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);

    $countSql = "
        SELECT COUNT(*) AS total
        FROM reports r
    ";
    if (!empty($where)) {
        $countSql .= " WHERE " . implode(" AND ", $where);
    }

    $countStmt = $pdo->prepare($countSql);
    foreach ($params as $key => $value) {
        $countStmt->bindValue($key, $value);
    }
    $countStmt->execute();
    $total = (int)$countStmt->fetchColumn();

    return [
        'total' => $total,
        'filters' => [
            'status' => $status,
            'category' => $category,
            'date_from' => $dateFrom,
            'date_to' => $dateTo,
        ],
        'data' => $rows,
        'sql' => $sql,
    ];
}
